statement.markdown: Implement a binary search function to find the index of a target value in a sorted array. The function should return -1 if the target value is not found. The binary search algorithm should divide the search space in half at each step until the target value is found or the search space is empty.
statement.inputFormat: A sorted array of integers and a target value as input.
statement.outputFormat: The index of the target value in the array, or -1 if not found.
statement.notes: The input array is guaranteed to be sorted in ascending order.

constraints.description: The number of elements in the array
The input array sorted in ascending order
The target value to be searched

examples.explanation: The target value 5 is found at index 4 in the array.
The target value 6 is not found in the array.

hints.text: Start with the implementation of binary search in a sorted array.

editorial.title: Correct Implementation of Binary Search
editorial.intuition: The key to binary search is to divide the search space in half at each step until the target value is found or the search space is empty.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves checking each element in the array one by one, which is inefficient for large arrays.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a binary search algorithm to find the target value in logarithmic time.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the binary search algorithm can be proved by induction on the size of the search space.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the binary search algorithm.
commonMistakes: Not handling the edge case of an empty array

functionSignature.python: def binarySearch(self, arr, target: int) -> int:
